channel.title: Help: Research and Resources (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Help: Research and Resources" - Doing background research to use real-life knowledge to make in-universe articles sound professional.

item.title: What temperature can pizza be safely eaten?
item.description: It's for an scp article I'm making
item.content:encoded:
I'm making a scp article involving a pizza. This pizza can maintains its heat but I'm trying figure out what temperature to adjust it to so people can safely consume it without burning their tongue. I tried looking it up but all it said is how the cook pizza in the oven.

So in your opinion, what temperature does a pizza have to be safely eaten without being burned and being tasty?

item.title: Aboriginal Australian Culture and Dreamtime
item.description: Looking for stories about koalas, Dreamtime lore, personal experiences.
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone! I am currently writing an SCP concerning the disappearance of koalas due in part to the Dreamtime Serpent, and I am particularly looking for info concerning koala and Dreamtime lore, as well as personal experiences with regards to these cultures.

I would also like to get info on what ceremonies, lifestyle measures, and traditions are primarily practiced nowadays for environmental purposes. If possible, a personal experience with them, particularly in events such as bunya gatherings, will be valuable :)

Thank you to those who will provide information on these matters!

item.title: Crime Scene Investigation Procedure
item.description: Need info on US crime scene procs
item.content:encoded:
Hey howdy hey, so I'm writing a tale at the moment that predominantly revolves around a crime scene. Thing is, I don't actually know very much about how actual crime scene procedures work, as the most popular point of reference tends to be dramatized TV shows.

I realise that in order to keep stuff interesting I'll need to strike a decent balance between real-world procedure and something a little more fictitious, but I'm not entirely sure where to look for real-world references. I've done some research, but I end up with a mixed bag of results that refer to how various countries handle it.

I'm looking for details of crime scene procedure as it would be in the real world particularly for the US - what kinda staff will be present, who's allowed where, how much can be done on-site that would contribute to the investigation, etc.
The crime takes place indoors (an apartment), and involves a dead body, if those details matter at all.

Articles for reference would be good, and if there's any online short story type deals that exist out there (I've had no luck finding any that aren't books) that would be fantastic.

item.title: World War Two Correspondence
item.description: Looking for a good resource to find letters home
item.content:encoded: Hi all! Wondering if anyone had a good source to find letters home from WWII. Doing a bit of resource to get a more historically accurate tone and style of writing for a tale I'm doing. (Fingers crossed) Stuff from the Korean War would do fine as well. It's not a top priority request, but if anyone has any experience in the subject any pointers would be great.

item.title: I'm writing a SCP about a reality bending fish
item.content:encoded:
It's about a fish that, when eaten, gives the person reality bending capabilities but kills them in a month. What are some good containment chamber ideas, especially if it's going to be in an aquarium?

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

What type of aquarium should I have, what do I feed them, what's in the water, do I give them plants? I've never had a fish in my life so I'm just asking what should be in the containment chamber?

item.title: I'm writing a SCP about a reality bending fish
item.description: What are some good containment chamber ideas, especially if it's going to be in an aquarium?
item.content:encoded:
It's about a fish that, when eaten, gives the person reality bending capabilities but kills them in a month. What are some good containment chamber ideas, especially if it's going to be in an aquarium?

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

What type of aquarium should I have, what do I feed them, what's in the water, do I give them plants? I've never had a fish in my life so I'm just asking what should be in the containment chamber?

item.title: does the human brain make a sound?
item.description: does a brain make a sound like the heart does? what does it sound like?
item.content:encoded: for an SCP I'm working on that's a living insane asylum. there's a thing where the foundation reach the asylum directors office and can't open the door, but they can hear a sound that's akin to the sound a brain makes (whatever that is).

item.title: Psychological Theories in 1800s
item.description: For a therapy SCP who was originally trained in the 1800s, what theories and practices would they most likely adhere to had they been trained around this time?
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is a reality-affecting entity that was captured by an English Earl (or baron, I'm unsure) at some point in the 1800s, probably around 1850 or later. I think the upper limit should probably be 1900 when the British nobility was dying off.

The earl in question had an interest in Psychology, and when he captured the anomalous entity he realised it's potential to help cure his patients. He slowly reformed the entity over time to genuinely come to enjoy helping humans, and it continued when he died.

A problem is is that the SCP mostly focuses on Therapeutic techniques, which from what i can gather came about more when Freud entered onto the scene, which i think was 1880 or so, which is a little early i think. It is also set in Britain, so it would be whatever theories were popular there.

So I'm currently unsure what theories of treatment this entity may subscribe to, as it would depend on which theories were around when it was being trained under this earl.

item.title: found a potential resource on soil science
item.description: a UN report that's 618 pages long. >.>
item.content:encoded:
http://www.fao.org/documents/card/en/c/cb1928en

its a report on soil biodiversity and soil health. i found it while working on an SCP, trying to figure out how to slim down the logs. Its over 618 pages long though… I'm probably going to need to use it as a reference book rather than a current read.

item.title: SCP Fuel: The Kentucky Meat Shower
item.description: It's exactly what it sounds like and I have no idea how we don't have an article about it
item.content:encoded:
Came up in chat, thanks to Edna_Granbo for serving as the progenitor of the weird-ass discussion that followed.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kentucky_meat_shower

item.title: Old-Styled Televisions?
item.description: I'm looking for a specific television model to use for my scp.
item.content:encoded:
My SCP is going to be an TV. I want it to be like those old thick square tv's that has antennas sticking out from the top.

Do any of you guys know a potential tv that'll work for that description?

item.title: Could a wormhole change your size?
item.content:encoded: Hello! I'm currently making an idea for an SCP article, and i had a question in mind, can wormholes change the size of the mass of anything that traspasses them?

item.title: German translation please?
item.description: Just a handful of short phrases, will read drafts or concepts and credit in return
item.content:encoded:
I’m making an image for my German anart SCP based on the journals they would find with it, and it would really help to have some sad/crazy German ramblings, as that would help sell the story. I could use Google but that wouldn’t be accurate, and anyone who knew German would possibly downvote. I’m very willing to read and critique drafts or whatever in return, but I can’t greenlight anything.

Here are some bits I have in mind:

  • Too creepy?
  • I don’t belong here
  • happy (just confirming, it’s glücklich, right?)
  • I just want it to be over
  • Pay electric bills!
  • The smile haunts me…
  • Let me out of this life
  • When will the night come?
  • cuter proportions = happier, but might not work
  • Am I going crazy?

item.title: Youtube Channels Inspiration List (Work In Progress)
item.description: List of channels that can provide inspiration for SCP stories and writing in general.
item.content:encoded:
There are a great number of Youtube channels that produce content that can be really useful and inspirational when you work on SCPs and tales. I've collected quite a few myself over the years, so I thought it would be useful to make a list of Youtube channels that can help us write.

What I really want is contributions. I'm going to start the list, but I want to expand it as much as possible. I'm going to use a format that will make it as fast as possible for people to find what they're looking for. If you contribute, please use the following format:

1. Include a link to the channel (preferably the channel main page, not just a video from the channel).
2. The name of the channel.
3. The category that they channel fits in (see categories below, might add more as time goes).
4. Description of the content on the channel. Try to include a little bit about who might be interested in the channel. See below for a framework.

Also note: The channel has to be somewhat directly relevant to writing SCPs. SCPs focus on phenomena that deviate from the norm. The list shouldn't include channels that delve into more mundane topics, even if those topics might be relevant to a story. It might be useful to research how a diesel engine works if you're writing a SCP focused on that topic, but it's not going to be even remotely useful to most people who write SCPs.

+ show block
– hide block

Youtube Channels

General Writing / Storytelling

Diana Callahan - Quotidian Writer
Link
Diana Callahan is focused exclusively on writing, with an emphasis on story, plot, dialogue and character.
Who is this for? Diana Callahan is a very well-rounded channel about writing that can probably teach every aspiring writer something.

Just Write
Link
Just Write focuses on looking at popular works of fiction, primarily movies, and trying to decipher what good lessons can be learnt from the work to improve your own writing.
Who is this for? Anyone who wants to use popular works of fiction as a foundation to improve their own writing.

Lessons from the Screenplay
Link
Lessons from the Screenplay, obviously, focuses on screenplays, often focusing each video on a single movie, going over the screenplay to explain what makes it so effective. Has a lot of videos that focus greatly on dialogue.
Who is this for? Anyone looking to cinema for tips on how to build tension and write beautiful dialogue.

Terrible Writing Advice
Link
Terrible Writing Advice features much shorter videos than most of the channels listed here that provide writing advice, rarely exceeding 10 minutes. The channel has an emphasis on catchy and entertaining writing advice, so the amount of information you get is a bit less than that of other channels, but it can be easier and more entertaining to follow.
Who is this for? Anyone looking for writing advice, who find normal writing channels a bit too slow and tedious.

SCP specific content

Evan Royalty
Link
Channel that produces longer movies based on the Foundation and SCPs. Has longer videos of outstanding quality.
Who is this for? Everyone. There isn't that many movies on the channel due to the effort and time put into each, so you need to see them and give them an upvote.

The Exploring Series
Link
To many on these forums, The Exploring Series was the gateway drug to the rest of the SCP community. Each video goes over a single SCP, telling the tale of the SCP in an interesting and captivating way, combining the story with images appropriate to it.
Who is this for? Everyone, but especially people who are yet not that familiar with the SCP foundation.

TheVolgun
Link
TheVolgun is a channel a bit in the same vein as The Exploring Series, presenting SCPs through narration and some appropriate images. Good production value.
Who is this for? People looking to get familiar with more SCPs, who don't read that much.

Real life - Investigation and policework

JCS - Criminal Psychology
Link
JSC focuses on the psychology of criminals, using interrogation videos as his primary source of information.
Who is this for? This channel is an invaluable asset if you want to create realistic interrogation scenes in your writing.

Real Life - Psychology

Dr. Todd Grande
Link
Dr. Todd Grande focuses on making psychological profiles of real-life murders and serial killers. Some of his profiles focus on other interesting people who are somehow outliers of society.
Who is this for? Anyone looking to create much more realistic and grounded characters, especially evil or malevolent characters.

Real Life - Philosophy

Academy of Ideas
Link
A channel that goes into great detail on some of the darker writers in philosophy and psychology. The channel explores archetypes, shadow projection, despair and similar topics.
Who is this for? Anyone who wants to learn more about the darker philosophers like Carl Jung, Nietzsche and Kirkegaard.

Real Life - Mysteries and peculiar phenomena

Lemmino
Link
Channel that focuses primarily on real life mysteries, like the Bermuda Triangle or Dyatlov Pass Incident.
Who is this for? Anyone looking to find information on the real life mysteries that often inspired SCPs.

What If
Link
What If produces short videos focused on exploring hypothetical scenarios like "What if you were swallowed by a whale?" or "What if our universe collided with another one?".
Who is this for? Anyone interested in hypothetical scenarios as a foundation for their SCP.

Fiction - Horror

Acolytes of Horror
Link
Acolytes of Horror specializes in making fewer, but fairly long and in-depth analyses of horror. Primarily he analyses movies, but some of his videos focus on the historic influences on modern-day horror.
Who is this for? Anyone looking to really delve deep into the psychology and symbolism of effective horror.

The Vile Eye
Link
The Vile Eye makes psychological profiles of famous evil characters, from Nurse Ratchet in One Flew Over The Cuckoo's Nest, to Michael Meyers from Halloween.
Who is this for? Anyone who wants to create a memorable evil character.

Ryan Hollinger
Link
Ryan Hollinger makes videos where he analyzes various horror movies, discussing what makes them effective.
Who is this for? Anyone looking to widen their background knowledge on horror movies.

Fiction - Thriller and Drama

Fiction - Science Fiction

DUST
Link
Amazing channel that produces high-quality short sci-fi movies, each with stories that could easily have been fitted inside the SCP universe.
Who is this for? This is one of the few channels here that I would recommend for everyone, but in particular anyone interested in the short sci-fi format.

Isaac Arthur
Link
This channel focuses on hard sci-fi topics such as Dyson Spheres and Matrioshka Brains as well as hypothesizes about aliens. A lot of the material might be a bit too large in scale to fit into the SCP universe.
Who is this for? Anyone who's interested in hard sci-fi and civilizations on a galactic scale.

John Michael Godier
Link
The creator of this channel is a science-fiction writer who focuses on hard sci-fi, with an emphasis on realism. The content describes a lot of hypothetical science fiction phenomena.
Who is this for? Anyone who's interested in hard sci-fi, especially concepts such as multiple universes and alien civilizations.

Like Stories of Old
Link
This channel is very philosophical, it goes into deep detail on topics such as time travel, the hero's journey, global pandemics and empathy. It can help create an emotional and philosophical underpinning to any story you tell.
Who is this for? Anyone who wants to learn more about how sci-fi concepts and story tropes connect to a deeper philosophical and psychological truth.

Quinn's Ideas
Link
Quinn's Ideas focuses very heavily on the Dune universe, and as such goes into tremendous detail on topics such as psychic powers, genetics and omniscience. If you haven't read the Dune books, there are videos several hours long that go through each book. Has lately also started making videos on H.P. Lovecraft.
Who is this for? Anyone who wants to learn more about the Dune universe without reading all of the books. Also a great source of inspiration if you're writing on omniscience and related psychic powers.

Fiction - Urban Legends and Creepypasta

Dark5
Link
A short-form channel where various urban legends and creepypasta are vividly explained with accompanying images.
Who is this for? Mainly for those interested in the creepypasta format.

Top 5 Scary Videos
Link
Top 5 videos discussing monsters and scary stories taken from both fiction and urban legends. Features creations from several of our SCP contributors.

Audiobooks

HorrorBabble
Link
Has all of the H.P. Lovecraft books, as well as many other horror writers such as Robert Bloch

As a courtesy to our mobile users, please collapse long forum posts. ~ McB

item.title: Interesting destructive skips?
item.description: I guess this is where I put it?
item.content:encoded:
I'm making an article that's about a seemingly indestructible toothpaste that reacts strangely with other contained anomalies. I'll link it for context (First tab, addendum 1). As you see, I've already added SCP-1012 and a dado log. I'm looking for other reactive/ destructive SCPs that I could add to the logs.

Notes:
Please don't send me 106, 682. I shouldn't even have to mention this, but some people are what they are.
If possible, I'm not looking for brute force/ "Lazer beam pew pew" type things. Uniqueness is what I crave for.

Thanks in advance.

P.S: I'd prefer underrated/ not extremely popular skips because y'know. Those already have enough recognition.

item.title: Anyone here familiar with Dissociative Disorders?
item.description: I'm creating an scp with D.I.D. can anyone help me give some experiences?
item.content:encoded: I'm making an scp thats a human being with D.I.D. and the alters have supernatural powers. I myself suffer from D.I.D. and am basing the dissociation off of my own personal experience. Still, I know that systems are different from person to person, and am wondering if my own experiences clash with anothers, thus invalidating the authenticity of the scp's main anomalies (meaning the superpowered alters). Would anyone here be willing to help me research dissociation and/or give examples of experiences within your (plural) systems?

item.title: Translating C++ Syntax to Real Life
item.content:encoded:
Right off the bat this is going to sound nonsensical, but I need help writing some code using a fictional (anomalous) coding language based on C++.

For example, I'd want a code that would clean my house when executed. Would the most "realistic" route be to define the house as a variable and go into an if statement for dirt / dust within the house?

Sorry, I know this makes no sense.

item.title: Anybody with cave mapping experience?
item.description: For an SCP that's a cave. For the life of me I can't figure out how the software works.
item.content:encoded:
I'm currently writing an SCP that's based off of the 'call of the void' phenomenon, specifically within a cave. I wanted to include a map of the first part of the cave in the description (it becomes inexplorable after a certain point due to its cognitohazard effects) but I have zero experience making cave maps so I thought it would be better to ask for help from somebody with more experience.

Any advice or assistance is welcome, as chances are it's a lot easier than I think it is.
The cave starts off with a drop, then fairly horizontally before dropping into an infinitely deep pit.

Cheers, - Glanda

item.title: Dactylic structure for English
item.description: Help with Dactylic structure in poetry.
item.content:encoded:
So I’ve been trying to write the poem that a I proposed quite awhile ago. It’s supposed to be like the Iliad retelling, so I wanted to try and imitate the structure. So I looked and it’s written in Dactylic Hexameter. I sort of remember it from poetry and classical literature but I wasn’t sure exactly how it went. So I looked it up and it seems to be ALMOST like iambic. Actually not really.

From reading and looking at videos, they describe it as a measuring the LENGTH of syllables, not the STRESS like Iambic. Also it’s measured almost like music with 4 beats per meter (or dactyl in this case.) Because it has two variations: one long and two short, or two longs (almost like short being quarter notes and long being half notes.) Iambic though is just unstressed then stressed.

The problem I’m having is that every example seems to be latin or Greek (which makes sense. Although it was the Aeneid that was Latin not the Iliad. At least I’m sure.) I think the English version of the Iliad isnt Dactylic, which would be hard to imitate in translation I’m sure. and I haven’t seen any actual English examples of Dactylic and honestly am having a hard time with judging between syllable length and syllable stress, because they almost seem the same to me. It’s easier when they used the Latin ones, because it seemed like their was almost a natural length to them (I think because Latin has a more rhythmical language, Greek seems almost like that too, I think?) So I’m not sure what I’m necessarily asking but I’m just saying I’m having a hard time with this thing.

item.title: Ergospheres and Black Hole Stuff
item.content:encoded:
Hey there, I'm a new researcher working on a very futuristic scip, and I'm looking for people who have a large understanding of the very things we don't understand: black holes. What would happen if a ship maintained orbit around a spinning supermassive black hole? What if somebody was to broadcast a message in the ergosphere? What if something faster than light flew towards a black hole?
I have too many questions. Also, what are professional terms surrounding these things, and how would you theoretically destroy one? Also, if you have similar questions, ask them here.
